condition:
Viewed from above and slightly to one side, the assembly presents as a low-profile, organic, pebble-shaped enclosure with a visible circumferential parting line dividing an upper and a lower dome, and a thin disc-like element visible at the seam. The layout is tagged as a linear chain along the Y-axis, reflecting the vertical stacking of the three unique, single-instance parts. The base cover (part 4607dfae) is the largest body at roughly 303.9 × 159.5 × 72.0 mm with an aspect ratio of 4.22; its geometry comprises 9 B-spline surfaces forming the curved walls and 31 planar faces for the flat base and small embossed top-deck details, plus 4 cylindrical faces at a radius of 6.0 mm with an 8.0 mm axial extent that correspond to a small circular boss or recess on the top deck. The housing cover (part 461c9fae) is somewhat smaller at 227.4 × 119.7 × 50.0 mm (aspect ratio 4.55) and is overwhelmingly freeform, with 292 B-spline faces and 19 planar faces; its upper surface features an oval recessed panel containing five small circular-edge dot features and several embossed relief symbols arranged in a cross pattern, clearly visible in the render. The cover plate (part 468e7438) is an extremely thin oval disc at 241.82 × 126.86 × 6.0 mm (aspect ratio ~40:1) with only 3 faces — two opposing planar faces and one B-spline rim — and no holes, bosses, or other features. No contact edges of any surface-pair type were recorded in the assembly graph, and no hole-link inferences exist because none of the three parts carry any through-holes or blind holes. All three parts are individually grounded. The absence of any detected mating interfaces suggests the parts nest concentrically by geometry alone, possibly retained by snap-fit detents or adhesive bonds that are not modeled as contact surfaces in the CAD data.

Parts:
- base_cover: A smooth, elongated, pebble-shaped dome with an oval footprint roughly 304 mm × 159 mm × 72 mm (aspect ratio ~4.2). Its shell is formed by B-spline surfaces curving from a thin lower rim to a broad flat top deck, which carries a small circular feature at radius 6.0 mm and embossed surface reliefs. All 31 planar faces share ±Y normals, and no through-holes or assembly contact pairs are present. The part is grounded and appears to likely serve as a stationary cover, cap, or base element.
- housing_cover: A smooth, elongated dome-shaped solid approximately 227 mm × 120 mm × 50 mm (aspect ratio 4.55), composed almost entirely of freeform BSpline surfaces (292 of 311 faces) with 19 planar faces oriented strictly up or down. An oval recessed panel with small embossed relief features is visible on the top surface; five circular edges correspond to small dot-like features within this panel. The part has no holes, no cylindrical faces, and no recorded assembly contacts, suggesting it likely serves as an outer housing cover or enclosure shell that could mate to a lower body through snap-fit or similar means.
- cover_plate: A very thin, flat, oval-shaped solid measuring roughly 242 × 127 × 6 mm with an aspect ratio of about 40:1. It consists of just two parallel planar faces (top and bottom) joined by a smooth B-spline peripheral rim — only 3 faces total — with no holes, bosses, or cylindrical features whatsoever. The two large flat faces with opposing normals represent the primary potential mating surfaces. Given its featureless, thin disc form, this part likely serves as a cover plate or closure element that could seat flush against or into a corresponding oval recess.

Annotation Caveats:
No contact edges of any type were detected between any pair of parts, despite the three shells clearly being designed to nest together. All three parts are independently grounded, so there is no parent-child kinematic chain. No holes or fastener features exist on any part, meaning retention mechanisms (snap-fits, adhesive, friction) are not captured in the geometry. The 4 cylindrical faces on the base cover (r = 6 mm) may represent a locating boss but no corresponding mating feature was found on the housing cover. The embossed text and icon reliefs are modeled as surface features only, without volumetric detail.
